rulings.Case Title: KERRY MANSFIELD VS. BROTHERS INTERNATIONAL HOLDING CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm 0otion To Sever Complaint And X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2014, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT WORLD GOLF TOUR, INC'S Motion To Sever Complaint And Cross-Complaint Granted. The issues involved in the underlying action between Plaintiff and Defendant-Brothers International are distinct from those involved in the action between Brothers International and Cross-Defendant World Golf. Moreover, a finding that Brothers International was not negligent in the underlying action would negate the need for any participation by World Golf. Alternatively, Brothers International would not be prejudiced by a subsequent trial determining World Golf's liability as it would be based on the wholly distinct issue of express contractual indemnity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: ELLEN PAO VS. KLEINER PERKINS CAUFIELD & BYERS LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Moiton To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Pltf'S First Reque3st For Produdction
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ELLEN PAO's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Plaintiff's First Request For Produdction. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The hearing focus will be the right-of-privacy "direct relevance test" mandated by Board of Trustees v. Superior Court (1981) 119 CA3d 516. The Court is also concerned about the overbroad specific language of some of the discovery categories and the extent to which (if at all), the specific category language was narrowed during the "meet and confer" process. The defendant shall produce at the hearing courtesy copies of its Opposition and supporting documentation along with a copy of the Confidentiality/Protective Order applicable to the action. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROMAN HULSEY et al VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT WELLINGTON POWER CORPORATION (ERRONEOUSLY SUED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer to the 2nd Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend because the Superior Court lacks jurisdiction to entertain the instant suit pursuant to Cal. Public Utilities Code sec. 1759, San Diego Gas & Electric Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1996) 13 Cal.4th 893 ("Covalt") and Ford et al v. Pacific Gas and Electric Co. (1997) 60 Cal.App.4th 696. See CCP 430.10(a). In San Diego Gas & Electric Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1996) 13 Cal.4th 893, 917-918 ("Covalt"), the court provides that the CPUC has exclusive jurisdiction where "(1) the commission had the authority to adopt a regulatory policy; (2) the commission has exercised its authority; and (3) the superior action would hinder or interfere with the commission's exercise of regulatory authority." Id. 13 Cal.4th 917-918. Here, the defendants correctly note that the CPUC has the authority to adopt SmartMeter policy under Public Utilities Code secs. 768, 451, 761, and 762. The CPUC exercised its authority, allowed the installation of the Smart Meters, and concluded that they were safe and in compliance with FCC regulations. Allowing this action to proceed would interfere with the CPUC's regulatory authority. Plaintiff argues that the action is proper under Cal. Public Utilities sec. 2106. As Ford et al v. Pacific Gas and Electric Co. (1997) 60 Cal.App.4th 696 explains, sec. 2106 has been limited to those situations in which an award of damages would not hinder or frustrate the CPUC's declared regulatory policy. Sec. 1759 bars an action under sec. 2106 not only when an award of damages would directly contravene a specific decision of the CPUC, but also when an award would simply have the effect of undermining a general regulatory policy. The CPUC has determined that SmartMeters can be installed and pose no undue safety risk. Plaintiff's suit clearly attempts to undermine that policy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EG

rulings.Case Title: HECTOR PAZOS VS. CASA THAI MARKET, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS CASA THAI MARKET, INC. AND HUNG LI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled. Allegations that an event occurred "on or about" the crucial date for statute of limitations purposes overcome a general demurrer. It is enough that the claim may be timely. (Childs v. State of California (1983) 144 CA3d 155, 160.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN, INC, KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC. (Redacted) Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action; Off calendar pursuant to the parties' stipulation. The Court will execute the stipulation and order submitted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/EG

rulings.Case Title: GUY O. KORNBLUM VS. GENESIS SPECIALTY TILE & ASSOCIATES LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 10, 2014, Line 14, Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment as to Defendant Eleftherios "Ted" Efstratis. Denied. (CCP § 483.010.) Plaintiff has neither demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the merits nor that the attachment is for a fixed or readily ascertainable amount. In the underlying action, Plaintiff was discharged after partial performance. Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the fees sought are reasonable. (Cazares v. Saenz (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 279, 287.) Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the attachment is for a fixed or readily ascertainable amount. The evidence proffered by Plaintiff is conclusory. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EG
